Sentence: The patient is a 2-month-old female with a 9-day history of illness. Per mom's report, the illness started 9 days ago with a dry cough.
Notes: Infant under 3 months with 9-day symptom history warrants close monitoring

Sentence: the patient was given Xopenex 2 puffs every 4 to 6 hours for home regimen
Notes: Outpatient bronchodilator regimen; standard asthma/wheeze management

Sentence: A chest x-ray was obtained and was noted to be normal.
Notes: Normal chest X-ray; no actionable finding

Sentence: The patient presented to the emergency room last evening with approximately 7- to 8-day history of abdominal pain
Notes: Multi-day abdominal pain in ED presentation suggests investigation needed

Sentence: She had a CT scan at that time and she was told it was "normal."
Notes: Normal CT scan result; no acute finding detected

Sentence: She was given oral antibiotics of Cipro and Flagyl.
Notes: Oral antibiotic combination commonly used for gastrointestinal infection

Sentence: The patient presented with symptoms of chest pain
Notes: Chest pain is a red flag symptom requiring immediate cardiac workup

Sentence: This 5-year-old male presents to Children's Hospital Emergency Department by the mother with "have asthma."
Notes: Known asthma diagnosis reported by mother on presentation

Sentence: He has had some vomiting and abdominal pain
Notes: Two gastrointestinal symptoms together suggest acute abdominal cause

Sentence: The lung perfusion scan  demonstrated 47% flow to the left lung and 53% flow to the right lung
Notes: Quantified perfusion result; slight asymmetry between lungs warrants review

Sentence: This is a 27-year-old female who presents with a couple of days history of some dental pain.
Notes: Dental pain is localised and non-systemic; routine urgency unless signs of spread

Sentence: She has had increasing swelling and pain to the left lower mandible area today.
Notes: Progressive swelling of the mandible raises concern for spreading dental infection

Sentence: The patient complains of neck pain.
Notes: Neck pain as isolated complaint; no neurological red flags mentioned

Sentence: The patient is diabetic and has a prior history of coronary artery disease.
Notes: Two chronic diagnoses documented; cardiovascular history increases overall risk

Sentence: The patient presents today stating that his chest pain started yesterday evening
Notes: New onset chest pain since previous evening; cardiac cause must be excluded

Sentence: He describes the pain as a sharp and heavy pain which radiates to his neck & left arm.
Notes: Pain radiating to neck and left arm is a classic cardiac referral pattern

Sentence: The patient took 3 nitroglycerin tablets sublingually over the past 1 hour
Notes: Sublingual nitroglycerine use indicates active chest pain episode in progress

Sentence: The patient is a 45-year-old male complaining of abdominal pain.
Notes: Abdominal pain as chief complaint; age and gender noted for clinical context

Sentence: Ms. ABCD is a 69-year-old lady, who was admitted to the hospital with chest pain and respiratory insufficiency.
Notes: Two serious presenting complaints on admission; immediate assessment required

Sentence: She has chronic lung disease with bronchospastic angina.
Notes: Chronic lung disease with bronchospastic component; ongoing monitoring expected

Sentence: we have started her on Coreg 6.25 mg b.i.d.
Notes: Beta-blocker initiated; starting dose suggests new prescription or titration

Sentence: Patient developed nausea and right upper quadrant pain, which apparently wrapped around toward his right side and back.
Notes: Right upper quadrant pain with nausea suggests possible hepatobiliary pathology

Sentence: Cultures taken from the wound on 06/25/2008, were reported positive for methicillin-sensitive Staphylococcus aureus (MSSA).
Notes: Positive wound culture confirms active infection requiring targeted treatment

Sentence: Patient started cyclosporine and prednisone on 03/30/10.
Notes: Immunosuppressant combination typical of post-transplant or autoimmune protocol

Sentence: She was admitted to the hospital from 07/11/10 to 07/14/10 with acute kidney injury.
Notes: Acute kidney injury is a serious confirmed diagnosis requiring immediate management
